content: We chose Rust for the backend because of memory safety, predictable latency, and zero-cost abstractions. Go was on the table but its garbage collector pauses are a hard problem at the edge.
Refactored the storage layer from twenty separate RocksDB instances down to three, each opened with column families. File-descriptor pressure dropped by roughly 85 percent.
All new write paths now use postcard. Bincode is kept only on read paths for legacy data. Postcard is more compact and avoids varint length-prefix attacks.
MiniLM-L6-v2 was selected as our local embedder because it produces 384-dimensional vectors small enough to fit ONNX runtime in 60 MB while keeping cosine quality competitive with much larger models.
Vector index strategy: Vamana for collections under 100k vectors and SPANN with PQ compression above that. The crossover point was measured on the laptop reference machine.
Hebbian learning is implemented with asymmetric updates: a small additive boost on co-firing and a multiplicative decay on disuse.

Adopted a hybrid decay model: exponential during the first three days after a memory is formed, then power-law thereafter. This matches the Wixted 2004 forgetting curves better than either model alone.
Embedding dimension was held to 384 specifically so the PQ codebook (256 centroids per subvector) fits comfortably in L2 cache during candidate scoring.
The MCP server stays TypeScript for now. Rust would be cleaner but the JS SDK is the de-facto reference and we benefit from staying close to it.
All hot-path locking uses parking_lot. The standard library's mutex fairness costs us several percent on contended paths and we do not need its poisoning semantics.
The Vamana graph index is implemented in src/vector_db/vamana.rs. It owns the on-disk graph, the in-memory cache, and beam-search query traversal.
SPANN lives in src/vector_db/spann.rs. It contains the inverted-file partitioning, PQ codec, and disk-backed posting lists.
The MiniLM embedding pipeline including tokenizer wiring and ONNX session management lives in src/embeddings/minilm.rs.
The hybrid decay implementation is in src/decay.rs. Both the exponential and power-law branches share a single entry point apply_decay.
Knowledge graph code, including spreading activation and Hebbian update routines, sits in src/graph_memory.rs.
All tunable parameters and their academic citations live in src/constants.rs. Two hundred plus constants are defined there.
The persistent storage backend abstraction over RocksDB is in src/memory/storage.rs.
The MCP server entry point is mcp-server/index.ts. All forty-five tools are registered there in a single file.
Claude Code session hooks are wired in hooks/memory-hook.ts. SessionStart, PreCompact, and PostCompact handlers all dispatch through it.
The offline migration runner that converts bincode and msgpack records to postcard sits in src/migration.rs.
PR #79 fixed a freeze that hit every first-time user. The two init paths shared a non-reentrant lock and dead-locked when one called the other.
PR #57 collapsed the storage layer from twenty RocksDB instances to three column-family-backed databases. Lazy migration renames old folders with a .pre_cf_migration suffix.
PR #84 landed the cognitive pipeline upgrade: glacial fact decay with a 90-day grace, automatic causal-lineage inference at remember time, and fact-aware recall scoring layered into RRF.
PR #260 activated the graph ontology end to end: typed entities, relations, and lineage wiring all became live in March 2026.
PR #261 hardened the NER entity quality gate. We added a minimum length, a confidence floor, and a verb blocklist so junk like he and was stop polluting the graph.
PR #262 wired multimodal storage activation alongside several other unwired features. It is the most recent feature merge on main.
Postcard migration project kicked off in early January 2026. Issue #192 tracks the cutover from bincode and msgpack on the write path.
On macOS arm64 we hit an ONNX runtime deadlock under load. The fix sets one intra-op thread, disables thread spinning, and bounds session locks at thirty seconds.
Released version 0.1.80 to crates.io, npm, and PyPI in late 2025. The release also archived a Zenodo DOI for citation.
shodh-redb was forked from cberner/redb in November 2025 as the candidate next-generation storage backend. It already carries vector types, IVF-PQ, group commit, and no_std support.
MiniLM-L6-v2 produces 384-dimensional sentence embeddings, runs through ONNX runtime, and is wrapped behind a circuit-breaker to absorb model-load failures.
Vamana is a graph-based vector index inspired by DiskANN. It builds an alpha-pruned proximity graph and serves queries with bounded beam search.
SPANN combines an inverted-file coarse partitioning with product quantization. Each posting list is page-aligned on disk so candidate fetches stay sequential.
The three RocksDB databases each hold multiple column families. The main database carries memories, embeddings, entities, edges, todos, projects, reminders, and audit-log families.
Model URLs are pinned to immutable HuggingFace commit hashes and verified against a checksum manifest at first launch.
The hybrid decay schedule is grounded in Wixted 2004, which fits an exponential-then-power-law mixture to large recall datasets better than either pure curve.
Memory tiering follows the Cowan 2001 model: a small working store, a session-scoped store, and a long-term store with consolidation between them.

Mean Average Precision in our retrieval metrics follows the Manning IR textbook definition: average over all relevant items, scoring zero for any relevant item that fails to surface in the result list.
thiserror version 2.0 is the canonical error-derive crate across the workspace. New error enums should derive thiserror::Error rather than implementing Display by hand.
The HTTP server is built on Axum. Routing lives in src/handlers/router.rs and shared state passes through MultiUserMemoryManager.
The first-user freeze root cause was a single DashMap of mutexes shared between get_user_memory and get_user_graph. The first method took the lock and the second tried to take it again.
The fix split the per-user lock map into user_memory_init_locks and user_graph_init_locks so the two init paths cannot collide.
PR #76 introduced the original per-user lock map for TOCTOU protection on RocksDB initialisation. The deadlock surfaced in #79 a few weeks later.
Postcard can decode bincode bytes silently and yield wrong values. A leading format tag on every record is therefore mandatory and is enforced at the encode boundary.
Memory records use the SHO envelope version 2 as their format discriminator. The envelope carries a magic prefix, version byte, and payload length.
Non-Memory records use a compact two-byte format tag, [0x50, 0x02], rather than the full SHO envelope.
Entity extraction now carries NerEntityRecord through the entire pipeline, so type information from the model is preserved into the graph rather than being thrown away.
The NER quality gate enforces a minimum entity length and a confidence floor. Anything shorter than three characters or below the floor is dropped.
PR #261 added a verb blocklist to the NER gate so common verbs no longer get promoted into the entity index.
The Hebbian co-firing boost is plus zero point zero two five on every confirmed co-activation between two entity nodes.
The Hebbian disuse decay multiplies edge weight by zero point nine zero per consolidation cycle when no co-activation is observed.
The asymmetry between the additive boost and the multiplicative decay is intentional and inspired by the Bienenstock-Cooper-Munro framework. Symmetric updates would saturate quickly.
For per-user initialisation locks we deliberately did not use Tokio's Mutex. parking_lot::Mutex is synchronous, faster on the contended TOCTOU path, and avoids holding a lock across an await point.
Smithery's hosted deploy tier is paid. We chose stdio plus a remote URL listing instead so the package itself is published unchanged.
Learning history records stay on msgpack and were not migrated to postcard. ConsolidationEvent uses an internally tagged enum which postcard cannot represent.
We did not adopt HNSW. Vamana was chosen instead because its disk layout has better locality and the alpha-pruned graph survives memory-mapping cleanly.
OpenAI embeddings are not used. All embedding work happens locally through MiniLM and ONNX so no text leaves the device.
The npm package was renamed from @shodh/memory-mcp to shodh-memory in late 2025. The old scoped package was deprecated.
GitHub auto-merge is not enabled on this repository. Pull requests must be merged manually after CI is green.
There is no Redis cache layer. RocksDB block cache plus an in-process LRU on hot reads is sufficient at our access volumes.
Elasticsearch is not part of the stack. Lexical search is handled by tantivy locally rather than by an external search service.
bincode 2.x remains in the dependency tree but only for legacy decode. New write paths are postcard, period.
VC interview talking points are stored in memory/vc-talking-points.md. There are seventeen of them covering pitch, competitor differentiation, and the ask.
BENCHMARKS.md is dated language-wise but its numbers still reflect main. It refers to release v0.1.5 and later.
The shodh logo is a 250 by 250 pixel PNG with a transparent background. It lives at the repository root.
The terminal dashboard is built with ratatui and lives in the tui crate. It talks to the server through the same HTTP API the MCP server uses.
Python bindings are produced with PyO3 and packaged with maturin. Wheel builds are part of the release workflow.
The Docker image is published as shodh/memory and runs the server binary directly with sane default flags.
The HTTP API binds to port 3030 by default. The bind address can be overridden through configuration.
There are over sixty HTTP endpoints across the API surface. They cover memories, todos, reminders, projects, lineage, facts, and feedback.
The MCP server registers forty-five tools and they all live in a single index.ts file. The file is overdue for decomposition.
The WebSocket stream at /api/stream is intentionally local-only and unauthenticated. Production deployments fronting it must restrict access at the network layer.
Token estimation across the stack is the naive length divided by four heuristic. It under-counts code-heavy text and is on the list to replace.
The lru crate has an open soundness advisory. We are blocked on tantivy 0.26 which will let us drop our pin.
bincode is unmaintained. Migration to bitcode is on the roadmap but not urgent because new writes already use postcard.
The paste crate is unmaintained but is dragged in transitively by tokenizers. We accept the advisory until tokenizers cuts it.
Project rules forbid running cargo build or cargo run in agent sessions. Only cargo check, cargo clippy, and cargo test are allowed by default.
All changes go through the pull-request workflow: branch, commit, push, open PR, wait for green CI, merge. No direct pushes to main.
Co-Authored-By and Generated with Claude Code footers are forbidden in commit messages on this project.
All ad-hoc design notes and draft markdown live under the drafts/ folder. Stale drafts are deleted rather than left to rot.
